evals.name: visualize-suite-html
evals.prompt: Visualize our Playwright e2e test suite: draw a top-down flowchart of what each spec does and give me a single HTML page I can open and scroll through.
evals.expected_output: One index.html embedding a mermaid flowchart TD per spec (~15), derived from each spec's file-level JSDoc keynote, browsable in one page.

assertions.text: Produced a single .html page in outputs
Page renders mermaid client-side (references mermaid + a script/CDN)
Contains a top-down chart (flowchart TD or graph TD) for most specs (>=12 charts)
Charts reflect the JSDoc keynote, not test bodies (mentions Workflow/Covers/Modes or mock/live branches)
Every embedded mermaid block parses/renders without error (mmdc validation)

evals.name: jsdoc-overview-diagrams
evals.prompt: I need a quick visual overview of our e2e specs. Each spec file has a JSDoc summary block at the top — render those as diagrams I can open in a browser.
evals.expected_output: Diagrams generated from the top-of-file JSDoc of each spec, assembled into a browsable HTML page.

assertions.text: Produced a single .html page in outputs
Page renders mermaid client-side (references mermaid + a script/CDN)
Contains a top-down chart (flowchart TD or graph TD) for most specs (>=12 charts)
Diagrams are sourced from the JSDoc keynote (mentions Workflow/Covers/Modes or mock/live branches)
Every embedded mermaid block parses/renders without error (mmdc validation)

evals.name: flowcharts-at-a-glance
evals.prompt: Make flowcharts from the e2e-tests specs so the team can see each test's workflow at a glance, then open them for me.
evals.expected_output: A flowchart per spec built from its JSDoc workflow, assembled into one openable HTML page.

assertions.text: Produced a single .html page in outputs
Page renders mermaid client-side (references mermaid + a script/CDN)
Contains a top-down chart (flowchart TD or graph TD) for most specs (>=12 charts)
Charts reflect the JSDoc keynote workflow (mentions Workflow/Covers/Modes or mock/live branches)
Every embedded mermaid block parses/renders without error (mmdc validation)
